answer:
The first statement is false because a subring can have a different unity (or no unity) than the larger ring; for example, in \(\mathbb{Z} \times \mathbb{Z}\), the subring \(\mathbb{Z} \times \{0\}\) has unity \((1,0)\) while the ring has unity \((1,1)\). The second statement is true, as every field is indeed an integral domain (it has no zero divisors). Therefore, the correct choice is C. False, True.

\boxed{C}
